title: agrostis huttoniae
intro: Agrostis huttoniae, also known as Hutton's bentgrass, is a species of grass native to New Zealand. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi. The health benefits of Agrostis huttoniae include its potential to aid in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Agrostis huttoniae can be made by infusing the dried grass in hot water, although further research is needed to fully understand its medicinal applications.
